commands.author: ragesaq

target.title: feat(realtime): add agent.progress ephemeral event (bot-write gated)
target.body:
## What this changes

This adds **`agent.progress`** as a first-class **ephemeral** event on the realtime
bus, establishing a **producer/consumer contract** between a *source bridge*
(producer) and realtime subscribers (consumers). It is a server-side transport +
authorization primitive. **No client rendering is included** — consumers (web/iOS
progress UI) build against it separately (see #24).

> **Contract note (updated):** the head of this branch uses the **existing bot-write
> authorization** for `agent.progress`, per maintainer change `8d09db0`
> (*"use bot write scope for agent progress"*). The earlier revision introduced a
> dedicated, non-inheriting `agent_progress:write` scope; that scope has been
> **removed** in favor of reusing `messages:write`, so a normal chat bot can publish
> progress without an extra grant step. The body, proof, and docs below describe the
> **current bot-write contract** at head.

### The relationship it introduces

Before this change, the ephemeral lane carried two human-shaped signals —
`typing.*` and `presence.changed` — and all durable agent output went through the
message store. There was **no transport for "an agent is mid-turn, here is what it
is doing right now."** This adds that third category and draws the trust boundary
around it.

| Role | Who | Capability | Enforcement |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Producer** | A **bot** with normal chat-write access | Publish `agent.progress` frames | `botTokenID` required (human session → **403**); a read-only bot → **403** |
| **Transport** | `POST /api/realtime/ephemeral`, `type:"agent.progress"` | Rides the existing ephemeral lane; never hits durable history | New branch in `publishEphemeral` |
| **Consumer** | Any subscriber with `realtime:read` who is a **member** of the target | Receives the frame over WS/SSE | Recipients **server-derived** from channel/DM membership |

### Three guarantees this primitive makes

1. **Bot-only, never a human session.** `agent.progress` requires a bot token; a
   human session is rejected with **403**. A read-only bot (no chat-write) is also
   rejected — publishing progress requires the same write capability as posting
   normal chat activity, so it is gated by the bot-write authorization the endpoint
   already enforces (`messages:write`). DM-scoped progress additionally requires
   `dms:write`.
2. **A private turn cannot leak workspace-wide.** A progress frame **must** name
   exactly one concrete surface (`channel_id` *xor* `direct_conversation_id`).
   Empty target → **400**; dual target → **400** — specifically so it can never fall
   through to the workspace-wide broadcast branch and spray
   thinking/command-output/patch detail to every member (Sentinel S1). Recipients
   are always **server-derived** from membership; the relay never names them.
3. **Ephemeral by construction.** Progress ticks share the typing/presence lane,
   so live "thinking / running a tool" activity never pollutes durable history.
   This is the deliberate split from the merged core PRs, which forward *durable*
   message-tool replies: core = the permanent record; this = the disposable live
   tick.

## Why there is no UI proof here

This is a machine-to-machine contract. Proof is an authz/privacy **test matrix**
plus a **WS delivery/scoping test** at current head — not a montage. (The rendered
consumer surface is proven separately in #24.)

**Authz matrix** (`TestAgentProgressEphemeralAuthz`, current head `8d09db0`):

| Caller | Frame | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Human session (no bot token) | channel `agent.progress` | **403** |
| Read-only bot (`bot:read`) | channel `agent.progress` | **403** |
| `bot:write` bot | channel target | **202** |
| `bot:write` bot | no target | **400** |
| `bot:write` bot | channel + DM target | **400** |
| `bot:write` bot | DM target | **202** |

**Wire scoping** (`TestAgentProgressDeliversOverRealtimeWithPrivateScoping`): a
channel-scoped frame reaches every channel member; a DM-scoped frame carrying
private reasoning reaches the DM member, while a non-member's socket never receives
it. Recipients are server-derived; the relay never names them.

Redacted current-head run (full output posted as a PR comment):

```
"POST /api/realtime/ephemeral" - 403   # human session
"POST /api/realtime/ephemeral" - 403   # read-only bot
"POST /api/realtime/ephemeral" - 202   # bot:write -> channel target
"POST /api/realtime/ephemeral" - 400   # no concrete target
"POST /api/realtime/ephemeral" - 400   # dual target
"POST /api/realtime/ephemeral" - 202   # bot:write -> DM target (scoped)
--- PASS: TestAgentProgressEphemeralAuthz (0.08s)
--- PASS: TestAgentProgressDeliversOverRealtimeWithPrivateScoping (5.28s)
```

## Files

- `apps/api/internal/httpapi/mutations.go` — recognize `agent.progress`; enforce
  bot-token-only + single-concrete-target on the existing bot-write path.
- `apps/api/internal/httpapi/agent_progress_test.go` — authz matrix + end-to-end
  WS delivery/privacy test (bot-write contract).
- `docs/features/bots.md`, `docs/features/realtime.md` — endpoint mapping and
  scoping docs.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Unreleased entry (added by maintainer in `8d09db0`).
target.author: ragesaq

commands.reason: re-review requires an open issue or PR

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor and maintainer follow-up because automation cannot supply current-head proof from the contributor's setup or choose the permanent auth contract.; Needs attention: The diff changes a realtime auth boundary and the public PR contract/proof still disagree with the final bot-write implementation.; Review finding: [P3] Remove the release-owned changelog entry — CHANGELOG.md:5 (sha=8d09db00492cd3fcfb21570f772e4697d69d6cc8)
commands.review_summary:
Adds `agent.progress` as a non-durable realtime event on `POST /api/realtime/ephemeral`, gated by existing bot message-write authorization plus bot-token and single-target checks, with WS scoping tests and docs.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. The remaining gaps are source/discussion-reviewable: the final head uses bot-write auth, the posted proof/body still describe a dedicated scope, and CHANGELOG.md line 5 violates release-owned guidance.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor and maintainer follow-up because automation cannot supply current-head proof from the contributor's setup or choose the permanent auth contract.
